missed_changes: Privacy emerged as his single most-emphasized theme — repeatedly called it critical and a 'non-negotiable'/dealbreaker for institutions (RAILGUN, Aleo, Aztec, Zcash, Seismic); the prediction did not mention privacy at all
Heavy focus on a token taxonomy and economic design — network vs company vs arcade tokens, fee switches, buyback-and-burn, and DUNA/BORG legal structures (Uniswap closed-loop) — not anticipated
Sustained concern that bridges don't deliver deterministic/exact amounts, which he argued breaks cross-chain crypto payments
New advocacy for clear-signing wallet UX and against blind signing as a security issue; also ERC-20R for theft recovery
Bitcoin surfaced as 'digital gold'/store of value (and a developer magnet); BTC was absent from the prediction
Reframed scaling/gas as essentially 'solved,' shifting the priority to UX, wallet design, and key management
notes: Nearly the entire sector/token/risk thesis was borne out in his own words and the a16z State of Crypto report he co-presented. DePIN scored absent because, unlike SOL (positive block-explorer tweet + report), it appears only in the firm report and never in his personal voice. Memecoin concern persists but is slightly softened ('interesting social trends,' lessons to learn). risk_on holds despite leverage caution after the Oct flash crash. Biggest gaps the prediction missed: privacy and the token-classification/economic-design agenda.
